description: Repair the relational middleware unit test harness so tests that execute `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware` under `UseRelationalBackend = true` provide a valid `MappingSet` before document extraction. Update `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Middleware/ReferenceArrayUniquenessValidationMiddlewareTests.cs` to reuse the same relational request setup pattern used by `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddlewareTests` or an equivalent shared helper. Acceptance: the reference-array uniqueness tests no longer fail with `MappingSet must be resolved before ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware when UseRelationalBackend is enabled`, and they continue asserting duplicate-reference validation behavior rather than setup failures.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the test output shows the duplicate-reference assertions passing and no `InvalidOperationException` from `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware`.

description: Introduce a shared canonicalization path for descriptor-valued identity members and use it consistently when building `DocumentIdentity` for both resource identities and reference identities. Refactor the relevant extraction code so descriptor-valued identity members are normalized to lowercase in one place, then apply that logic from `IdentityExtractor` and `ReferenceExtractor` without duplicating rules. Preserve existing descriptor-document canonicalization behavior and keep non-descriptor identity members unchanged. Acceptance: the same logical identity produces the same `DocumentIdentity` and `ReferentialId` whether it is extracted from a resource body or from a reference body, including descriptor-valued members such as `programTypeDescriptor`, `termDescriptor`, and `gradingPeriodDescriptor`.
steps-to-verify: Confirm descriptor-valued resource identity tests now expect canonical lowercase values and that reference/resource referential-id parity holds for equivalent logical identities.

description: Add focused regression coverage for descriptor-valued identity parity across extraction entry points. Extend unit tests so one scenario constructs the same target resource identity from a normal resource payload and from a reference payload, then asserts both the canonicalized `DocumentIdentity` contents and the resulting `ReferentialId` are identical. Include mixed-case descriptor URI inputs to prove canonicalization is case-insensitive for descriptor-valued identity members. Acceptance: the regression net fails if either extractor stops canonicalizing descriptor-valued identity members consistently or if referential-id generation drifts again.
steps-to-verify: Review the added tests and confirm they explicitly compare both `DocumentIdentity` values and `ReferentialId` values for equivalent mixed-case descriptor inputs.

description: Add an old PostgreSQL regression test that covers a document reference to a resource whose identity includes a descriptor-valued member, proving legacy reference validation uses the same canonical referential id as the stored target resource. Prefer an integration-level test in the old PostgreSQL path that exercises unresolved-reference behavior directly rather than relying on broad E2E fallout. Acceptance: a descriptor-valued reference that previously failed with `409 Unresolved Reference` succeeds when the referenced resource exists with equivalent identity values that differ only by descriptor URI casing, and the test would fail if reference/resource referential-id drift returns.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the new test demonstrates successful legacy reference resolution for a descriptor-valued identity scenario instead of a `409 Unresolved Reference` failure.

description: Re-run the targeted suites that cover the two known failure modes after the implementation is complete. Verify the unit-test harness regression is fixed, the identity canonicalization change does not regress relational write assumptions, and the legacy PostgreSQL regression is covered by a direct test. Acceptance: the targeted unit suites and old PostgreSQL regression suite all pass together on the branch, providing confidence before re-running GitHub Actions or broader E2E coverage.
steps-to-verify: If Docker-backed validation is needed afterward, tear down and re-set up the local DMS test stack before running broader PostgreSQL E2E coverage.
